commit.message:
Migrate email templates to MIT/OL theming (#115)

## Summary

Migrates Keycloakify email templates to use full MIT/OL branding,
replacing the MJML-based FTL templates previously maintained in
`ol-keycloak`. Templates are now React components using
`keycloakify-emails` and `jsx-email`, matching the visual identity of
the login pages.

Closes mitodl/hq#8794
See cleanup PR: mitodl/ol-keycloak#251

## Changes

### `src/emails/styles.ts` *(new)*
Shared inline style objects (`paragraph`, `ctaButton`, `fallbackUrl`)
extracted into a single module to avoid drift across templates.

### `src/emails/layout.tsx`
Rewrote `EmailLayout` with full MIT branding:
- `#F3F4F8` background, white card with `border-radius:8px`,
`max-width:600px`
- Header: MIT Learn logo (left) + MIT logo (right), separated by a
`#DDE1E6` border-bottom
- Adobe Typekit font (`neue-haas-grotesk-text`) via `<link>` in `<Head>`
(non-Outlook clients)
- Footer: realm name + 77 Massachusetts Ave, Cambridge address
- Uses jsx-email table components (`Row`, `Column`, `Img`) for Outlook
compatibility
- PNG logos referenced via `import.meta.isJsxEmailPreview` URL pattern
for correct asset resolution in both preview and production

### `src/emails/templates/assets/mit-learn-logo.png` *(new)*
PNG MIT Learn logo (300×55px) for email header. PNG used instead of SVG
for broad email client support (Gmail, Outlook).

### `src/emails/templates/assets/mit-logo-black.png` *(new)*
PNG MIT logo (45×24px) for email header.

### `src/emails/templates/email-verification.tsx`
Rewrote to use `EmailLayout` with MIT-branded content:
- "Verify Your Email" heading
- `exp("realmName")` in body text
- MIT red (`#A31F34`) CTA button linking to `exp("link")`
- Expiration notice and fallback URL

### `src/emails/templates/password-reset.tsx` *(new)*
Created missing password-reset template matching
`ol-keycloak/password-reset.ftl`:
- "Reset Your Password" heading
- MIT red CTA button
- Fallback URL

### `src/emails/templates/org-invite.tsx`
Simplified by removing brittle theme `if/else` branching. Now uses
`exp("realmName")` generically, so it works consistently for both
`ol-learn` and `ol-data-platform` themes.

## Design tokens used
| Token | Value |
|---|---|
| Background | `#F3F4F8` |
| Card bg | `#FFFFFF`, `border-radius:8px` |
| Header/footer border | `#DDE1E6` |
| Text | `#212326` |
| CTA button | `#A31F34` (MIT red) |
| Font | `neue-haas-grotesk-text, sans-serif` |

commit.message:
Silence extension process stderr logs to prevent call-stack-like error output (#6939)

* Silence extension process stderr logs to prevent call-stack-like error output

Extension processes emit verbose gRPC MessageBroker trace logs via
Go's log.Printf, which defaults to os.Stderr. Since azd captures
extension stderr and includes it in error messages, these internal
diagnostics appear to users as call-stack-like error output.

Silence the global logger in ExtensionHost.Run() by setting
log.SetOutput(io.Discard) unless AZD_EXT_DEBUG=true is set.
This ensures backward compatibility with extensions compiled
against older SDK versions that still use log.Printf directly.

commit.message:
consensus: Remove transient nodes from registry on disconnect

Free-tier (Handshake-registered) masternodes have no collateral stake
and are considered transient. On disconnect they are now fully removed
from the registry instead of merely marked inactive.

Previously, a node that joined and left would remain in the registry
with is_active=false for up to an hour (AUTO_REMOVE_AFTER_SECS=3600).
During that window, code paths using list_all() — specifically the
FallbackVote handler in message_handler.rs — would include the
disconnected node in total_avs_weight, inflating the finality threshold
and potentially stalling the chain.

Changes:
- mark_inactive_on_disconnect: Handshake nodes are removed from both
  memory and disk; OnChain (Bronze+) nodes are still marked inactive
  so they can reconnect without re-registering their collateral.
- message_handler.rs FallbackVote handler: list_all() -> list_active()
  so inactive OnChain nodes also cannot inflate vote weight or have
  their signatures accepted while disconnected.

The bitmap/reward invariant is preserved automatically: removed nodes
are absent from the registry at block-production time, so they cannot
appear in the next block's active-masternode bitmap and cannot receive
rewards for a block interval they did not complete.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: Coming Up Next + crash fix + debug mode

Three fixes in one commit:

1. Coming Up Next always showing 'End of playlist':
   Root cause — socat was unreliable for fetching the large playlist
   property (~38KB for 263 tracks). The socat -t timeout and raw pipe
   through jq produced empty results. Fix: switch to _jukebox_ipc
   (python with proper Unix socket handling and request_id matching)
   for the playlist property query. This is slower but correct, and
   only runs on full redraws (not the fast partial progress updates).

2. 'command not found: _jukebox_cleanup' crash:
   The EXIT trap and explicit _jukebox_cleanup call at end of the main
   loop caused double invocation — the second call failed because
   unfunction had already removed it. Fix: add idempotent guard via
   _jukebox_cleaned flag.

3. Debug mode (jukebox --debug / jukebox -d):
   Enables verbose logging to /tmp/jukebox-debug.log with timestamps.
   Logs mpv startup, socket wait, and Coming Up Next data extraction.
   Run 'tail -f /tmp/jukebox-debug.log' in another terminal to watch.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

refactor: remove reflection from all hot paths and reduce startup reflection (#1071)

- Add ClrType, DataIndex, HasSingletonFlag to DataFieldMetadata — captures
  PropertyInfo metadata at registration, eliminating runtime access
- Replace all field.Property.GetValue() with field.GetValueFn() (compiled
  delegates) across 6 files, ~10 call sites
- Replace all field.Property.PropertyType with field.ClrType across 12
  files, ~49 call sites
- SearchIndexing: TypeMetadata uses compiled getter tuples instead of
  PropertyInfo[]; prefers DataScaffold metadata over raw reflection
- AuditService: uses DataScaffold metadata + compiled delegates for
  change detection instead of per-save GetProperties/GetValue
- Data providers: ClearSingletonFlagsOnOtherRecords uses metadata-first
  path with compiled delegates
- ReportExecutor: FindAccessor returns compiled Func instead of
  PropertyInfo; eliminates GetValue reflection
- ServerLookupResolver: uses DataScaffold field metadata for FK traversal
  instead of GetProperties + GetCustomAttribute
- ActionExpander: removed reflection fallback for GetEntityKey
- RouteRegistrationExtensions: removed redundant GetCustomAttribute check
- MetadataSeeder: removed GetCustomAttribute check (all DataScaffold
  entities are annotated by construction)

Remaining reflection is startup-only (BuildEntityMetadata, EntityLayout-
Compiler, BinaryObjectSerializer TypeShape cache) — runs once per type
and results are cached. Zero reflection on any request hot path.

Fixes #1058

commit.message:
feat: implement dynamic GOT page allocation for unlimited TLS symbols

Resolves IMPROVEMENT_FINDINGS issue 1.6: GOT Size Exhaustion

Previously, the GOT (Global Offset Table) was limited to a single 4KB page,
allowing only 256 TLS symbol entries (16 bytes each). This hard limit caused
load failures for libraries with many TLS symbols.

Changes:
- Added GOTPages []uintptr to Object struct to track multiple GOT regions
- Modified allocateGOTEntryPair to dynamically allocate new 4KB pages as needed
- Updated Unload to properly cleanup all allocated GOT pages
- Added comprehensive test suite (loader/got_expansion_test.go):
  * TestGOTExpansion_MultiplePages: Validates 300 symbols across 2 pages
  * TestGOTExpansion_IdempotentAllocation: Ensures idempotent behavior
  * TestGOTExpansion_PageBoundary: Tests page boundary crossing

Impact:
- Libraries with unlimited TLS symbols can now be loaded
- No performance regression for libraries within first page
- Minimal complexity increase: allocateGOTEntryPair +1, Unload +1

Tests: All existing tests pass + 3 new GOT expansion tests

commit.message:
fix: soft steer falls through to hard steer on disposed JsonRpc connection (#293)

## Bug
`⚠️ Soft steer failed: Cannot access a disposed object. Object name:
'StreamJsonRpc.JsonRpc'`

When the underlying JsonRpc connection is disposed (e.g., CLI process
crashed), soft steer's `SendAsync` throws `ObjectDisposedException`.
Previously this just reported the error and gave up — the user's
steering message was lost.

## Fix
Connection errors (detected via `IsConnectionError`) in the soft steer
path now:
1. Remove the already-added user message from history (prevents
duplicate)
2. Fall through to the **hard steer** path (`AbortSessionAsync` +
`SendPromptAsync`)

`SendPromptAsync` has full reconnection logic: dispose old session →
restart server if needed → recreate client → resume session → retry
send. Non-connection errors still use the original error-and-cleanup
behavior.

## Tests
- Added `SteerSession_SoftSteerPath_ContainsConnectionErrorFallback` —
source-structure test verifying the fallback path exists with correct
patterns (`IsConnectionError`, `STEER-FALLBACK`, `History.RemoveAt`,
`softSteerSucceeded`).

## Verified
- All 2025 passing tests still pass (15 pre-existing failures unrelated)
- Mac Catalyst build succeeds (0 errors)

commit.message:
fix: converter patterns and exit code for improved compile rates

Fixes 7 root cause patterns that caused the C#→Calor converter to emit
syntactically invalid Calor that the parser rejected:

- P1: Null-conditional (?.) inside §C{} call targets — decompose to
  IF null-check + call
- P2: Method calls (§C{}§/C) inside IF conditions — hoist to temp
  bindings before IF (handles binary, unary conditions)
- P3: Array types emitted as [T] prefix — normalize to T[] postfix
  format that works in all parser contexts (cast, params, generics)
- P4: Nested §NEW inside §NEW args — hoist inner NEW to temp binding
- P6: String escape sequences \/, \uXXXX, \', \a, \b, \f, \v — add
  lexer support for passthrough and unicode escapes
- P8: Using alias emitted with = instead of : — fix CalorEmitter to
  use colon separator matching parser expectations
- Complex for-loop bounds with section markers (§IDX) — hoist to
  temp bindings to avoid breaking §L{} header parsing

Additional fixes:
- Exit code: calor CLI now returns non-zero on compile errors (was
  always 0 due to Environment.ExitCode vs ctx.ExitCode mismatch)
- Parser: ReadInlineTypeToken supports T[] postfix array syntax in
  function signatures

Test impact: All 237 conversion tests pass, all compiler tests pass
(7 pre-existing env-dependent failures unchanged).

Newtonsoft.Json benchmark: 49/57 files compile (86.0%), up from
42/57 (73.7%) — 7 files fixed.

commit.message:
fix: Coming Up Next was always empty due to playlist IPC failure

_jukebox_fast_get 'playlist' piped mpv's response through
'jq -r .data // empty', which pretty-printed the entire playlist
array as 1000+ lines of JSON. For large playlists (263+ tracks,
~38KB), socat -t 0.5 would timeout before receiving the full
response, resulting in empty pl_json and always showing
'End of playlist'.

Fix: bypass _jukebox_fast_get entirely for the playlist property.
Instead, make a single socat call with -t 2 (longer timeout) and
extract the next filename + item ID directly from the raw mpv
response via jq '.data[$idx]?.filename', avoiding the intermediate
pretty-printed variable altogether.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: implement git commands as MCP tools (#34)

Implement Epic 4 — Git Commands Implementation. Adds the git command
runner and tool builder that converts command config entries into
registered MCP tools.

New modules:
- src/git/runner.ts — spawns git processes with timeout and output capture
- src/git/tools.ts — converts CommandConfig to ToolDefinition with Zod
  input schemas, runtime guard validation, and CLI arg mapping
- src/git/index.ts — barrel exports

All 7 configured commands are now functional MCP tools:
- git_status: show working tree status
- git_log: show commit logs (configurable limit)
- git_diff: show diffs (staged, unstaged, between refs)
- git_branch: list branches (local or all)
- git_show: show commit or object by ref
- git_blame: show line attribution for a file
- git_remote: show remote repositories

Updated src/index.ts to wire config loading → tool building → registry.

Tests: 31 new tests (runner + tools), all 90 pass.

fix(blazor): fix WebSocket corruption from response compression + CSS error

Root cause: UseResponseCompression() wrapped the response stream BEFORE
MapRazorComponents/MapHub, corrupting WebSocket frames. Circuit connected
but immediately crashed: Server returned an error on close.

Fix: UseWhen(!IsWebSocketRequest) skips compression for WebSocket upgrades.

deprecated meta tag, enabled SignalR EnableDetailedErrors in non-prod.
